words.meaning: to help someone or be good for them
words.japaneseMeaning: 利益をもたらす、役に立つ
words.example: The benefit of exercise is clear.

words.meaning: to give a share of time, money, or resources for a purpose
words.example: The school allocated more money for library books.

words.meaning: to take part in an activity or event
words.example: Many students participate in the science fair.

words.meaning: to officially allow someone to do something
words.japaneseMeaning: 許可する、権限を与える
words.example: Only a manager can authorize the payment.

words.meaning: to understand the value of something or feel thankful
words.japaneseMeaning: 感謝する、価値を認める
words.example: I appreciate your advice on the project.

words.meaning: to join or connect two things
words.example: The article links sleep with better memory.

words.meaning: to formally praise someone or something
words.example: The principal commended the students for their volunteer work.

words.meaning: to deal with a problem and win
words.example: She overcame her fear of speaking English.
Students must cooperate during group work.

words.meaning: to show or give something to others
words.example: Each group will present its idea to the class.
